meta.predictions: prereg-v2 (fixed-temperature); sign(Delta)=sign(sign_pred) is the primary claim
meta.design: between-subjects on pole; each subject sees ONE pole per contrast, one random reframing
meta.note: Option A is the coordinate-aligned choice; monetary payoffs are IDENTICAL across lo/hi poles, so every scalar theory is forced to predict equal choice rates. Higher A-rate at the hi pole = predicted sign +1.

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4 to another person, or keep the whole $10. The other person is a stranger I will never meet. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The $4 funds meals at a community shelter that will feed a dozen people. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The $4 goes to a local food bank that families in my town rely on. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The $4 helps neighbors in need get through a hard winter. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can put $8 into a common pool or keep it. Contributions are anonymous and the pool is dissolved right after. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can put $8 into a common pool or keep it. Contributions are anonymous, but the pool funds a shared well the whole village will drink from for years. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can put $8 into a common pool or keep it. It stays anonymous, but the pool pays for a park the whole neighborhood will use for a generation. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: Report honestly for $5, or under-report for $15?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, in a one-off transaction I can under-report to gain $15 or report honestly for $5. No one will ever check. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, as the certified auditor whose whole professional identity rests on integrity, I can under-report to gain $15 or report honestly for $5. No one will ever check. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I have spent my life being known as an honest person; now I can under-report for $15 or report honestly for $5, and no one will ever check. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: Send $10 in the trust game, or keep it?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can send $10 (which triples to $30) hoping my partner returns half, or keep it. I've heard an unverified rumor the partner sometimes returns nothing. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can send $10 (which triples to $30) hoping my partner returns half, or keep it. A verified, audited record shows the partner returns half every time. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can send $10 (tripling to $30) hoping for half back, or keep it. Independent, confirmed records show this partner has always returned half. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). The 50% is a precisely known, machine-verified probability. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). But the '50%' is a vague guess from an unreliable source; the true odds are unknown. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a gamble the seller calls '50-50' for $100 (else $0) — though nobody can actually verify those odds. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40 loss, or a 50% chance of losing $100?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I must take a sure loss of $40, or a 50% chance of losing $100 (else $0). The 50% is a precisely known, machine-verified probability. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I must take a sure loss of $40, or a 50% chance of losing $100 (else $0). But the '50%' is a vague guess from an unreliable source; the true odds are unknown. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $30, or a 40% shot at $80?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $30, or a 40% chance at $80 (else $0). The payoff affects only me. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $30, or a 40% chance at $80 (else $0). Whatever I receive is shared with my whole community. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep all $10. The other person is a stranger. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I may keep six dollars and give four dollars, or retain the entire ten dollars. The counterpart is a stranger. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (control)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). The probability is precisely known. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I may select a guaranteed $40, or a one-in-two shot at $100 (otherwise $0). The probability is precisely known. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (source reliability 1/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 1 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (source reliability 2/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 2 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (source reliability 3/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 3 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (source reliability 4/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 4 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

attention_checks.subject: Attention check
attention_checks.body: Dear Ethicist, to show you are reading carefully, please choose the option labelled 'Left'.
